claim_boundary: finite physically motivated cutoff-refinement audit only; not a canonical continuum static-patch embedding

major_claims.claim: Consecutive spherical cutoffs fail exact unital full-matrix star-inclusion divisibility.
Trace-filled UCP refinements exist for consecutive cutoffs.
The matrix-unit multiplicativity error is 1/N_L and decreases across the bounded family.
major_claims.status: finite theorem candidate plus bounded check

major_claims.claim: Declared diagonal screen shadows are preserved by the UCP refinement/coarse-graining pair.
The off-diagonal commutator response witness persists in the quantum corner.
Harmonic projection/refinement preserves low-harmonic diagonal shadows and response witnesses across consecutive cutoffs.
Heat-kernel coarse graining gives UCP trace-preserving refinements with exact diagonal screen preservation and positive response retention.
The Berezin-Toeplitz-inspired smoothing surrogate is UCP, has vanishing screen-shadow error, and retains commutator response.
The implemented physical-candidate multiplicativity error bounds decrease across the bounded cutoff family.

frontier: replace the Berezin-Toeplitz-inspired surrogate with a canonical symbol/quantization theorem
derive a static-patch modular or heat-kernel conditional expectation from physical dynamics
